sdd-fleet
  • Brief
  • Boundary
  • Architecture
    • Overview
    • Workspace

    • Memory
  • Provenance
  • Pilot
01

The architecture: a control stack and its records

The architecture has two halves: a stack of three control layers — who acts — over the record planes where truth lives. At the centre is the workspace vault, the single source of truth that Jira, the registry and the catalog orbit. Each store owns exactly one kind of fact, because the classic failure is two stores claiming the same truth.

Every consequence lives in code; the model judges only the irreducible residue; a human owns the blast radius. Harness Model Human the one rule →

Layer 3 · workspace

The workspace — plan above, pull below Model Harness Human

A parent folder over the member repos — git submodules, one Obsidian vault. epic-plan (model + human) authors the dependency DAG and the contract design into workspace/.sdd/, ratified by a human before any work spreads to the repos. A developer then pulls the next ready story with next-story — a deterministic resolver over live status + the registry — and starts it. The conductor is the optional autonomous variant of that same resolver: a level-triggered reconciler — it re-reads live state every cycle instead of trusting memory — with no model in the loop. The widest-blast-radius layer is therefore the most deterministic, not the least. Planning is a command; dispatch is deterministic plumbing.

Layer 2 · per-repo

The feature machine — one per repository Model Harness

SPEC → REVIEW → FINALIZE → BUILD → CHANGE REVIEW → HANDOFF. A coded harness runs the phases and routes the work; the model only drafts and reviews within them, never writing source or reordering a phase. Single-worktree, so two changes never race in one repo.

Layer 1 · enforcement

Hooks — enforcement at the tool boundary Harness

The harness owns the sequence; the hooks own the boundary. They are fail-closed — a gate's default is to block, and opening it takes an explicit, coded allow, so a missing or unverifiable check stops the action rather than letting it slip through. They block a forbidden action however it is initiated — no source before the spec is finalized, no handoff on a failing or untraceable suite, no contract change without a published, version-bumped spec — catching any deviation the harness did not foresee, the model going off-script inside a phase included. This layer is why Claude Code specifically: hooks intercept at the tool level, in-session, so a forbidden write is blocked before it happens rather than commented on afterwards, and review runs in separate subagent lenses the drafting model cannot overrule. An assistant that can only suggest changes has no equivalent enforcement point.

In a bank's terms. These planes map onto controls a regulator already recognises:
Control a regulator recognisesThe sdd-fleet mechanism
Change-control evidence The replayable trail: gated .sdd/ artifacts, the pinned-SHA workspace commit, the registry's version history, Jira sign-offs — the audit sentence is re-derived from the record, not recollected.
Four-eyes / segregation of duties Epic & contract ratification before any fan-out; a HANDOFF approval forced by the computed blast radius (≥N consumers, or money_movement / pii data classes) — and the approval is pinned to the blast-radius signature, going stale if the radius widens.
Single system of record One fact, one store: the vault .sdd/ is the source of truth; Jira owns intent + status; the registry owns versioned contracts (append-only); the catalog is derived and recomputed.
Testing-evidence integrity Every acceptance criterion maps to a test before implementation (traceability gate); test paths are write-locked for the run so the oracle cannot be edited to pass; QA verdicts are grounded in captured coverage output, never the model's opinion of it.
Model-risk boundary The model never decides a consequence: gates, vote counting, routing, semver and blast-radius are code. Its judgement calls are enumerated and logged — drafting, per-criterion review, one soundness verdict per contested refutation, one "is this diff breaking?" call.
And the planes are likely tools you already run — Jira, a contract registry, a Backstage-style catalog, and a git-backed document store.

The whole system as one flow

The three layers and the record planes, traced end to end for a single change — from a business intent to a replayable audit trail. The detailed views expand the two shaded lanes: Layer 3 — the workspace and Layer 2 — inside one repository.

Each lane in detail: the workspace · the feature machine · and the record they share, the linked vault.

sdd-fleet — a Claude Code plugin · engineered for audit and replay
Deterministic harness · probabilistic generation · human accountability
